founder.notes: Cold-start score anchored on public execution, not pedigree absence. Independent GitHub cadence (1,400 commits, weekly releases, 86% founder-authored), hackathon win against ~400 teams, and launch-thread technical depth outweigh the Memory snapshot (71) slightly on the upside while keeping a wide band. Prior-employment remote-cache claim is deck-only—gate: one reference call to prior fintech infra lead confirming scope and outcomes. Shipping velocity is the thesis-aligned signal here; unverified résumé detail widens the band rather than pulling the point estimate down.

market.notes: Structural why-now is real—agent-driven CI loops inflate build minutes and spend—but the buyer already sits inside a crowded remote-cache layer (Bazel/Gradle remote cache, GitHub Actions cache, Turborepo/Nx). Ferrite's wedge (content-addressed dedup across agent fleets) is plausible but not yet proven as a standalone budget line versus incumbent caches absorbing the workload. Adoption proxies are early and contested: 340 downloads/week and 2,900 lifetime registry downloads (sig-004) plus GitHub/community pull (sig-002, sig-005) suggest nascent PMF, while deck scale claims (sig-001) remain unreconciled. Neutral until agent-CI spend is confirmed as incremental procurement, not a feature request to existing vendors.

idea_vs_market.notes: Content-addressed remote caching is a proven primitive; the agent-fleet dedup angle is a coherent extension, not a category invention. Real artifact exists (commits, releases, CI config, external contributors) and survived independent scrutiny (hackathon judges, launch commenters self-identifying as production users). Registry tension (sig-004) caps near-term scale claims but does not invalidate the mechanism—check that would settle idea-market fit at stated positioning: run reproducible 10x benchmark on a design-partner agent-CI workload with before/after cache hit rates and cost ledger. If benchmark holds at modest install base, idea survives as-is at seed scale; if not, team's shipping record (sig-002) supports pivot within devtools/infra rather than collapse.

disagreement: Founder and idea-vs-market both read positively on independently verified build-and-ship evidence (high-70s founder band, survives-as-is), while market stays neutral: structural agent-CI cost tailwind is real but incumbent cache vendors already own the budget line, and independent adoption proxies (sig-004) sit far below deck-claimed install scale (sig-001). Execution confidence outruns proven market capture—the axes disagree on whether verified shipping velocity is sufficient proof of wedge entry or whether contested commercial scale should cap market enthusiasm.

opposed_poles.strongest_argument: Independently verified product momentum—sustained commit/release cadence, hackathon validation, and launch engagement with identified production users—demonstrates a technical founder shipping a real devtools artifact into an emerging agent-CI cost problem without waiting for institutional capital.

Commercial credibility is in active tension: public registry lifetime downloads (2,900) fall below deck-claimed active installations (3,100), and all revenue/growth figures are subject-sourced only—suggesting traction narrative may be ahead of independently observable adoption.

routed_checks: Active installation definition (3,100) → request product analytics export with install vs. active-weekly-user breakdown, including self-hosted deployments not visible in public registry
Registry vs. deck install gap → ask founder to reconcile: enterprise/on-prem installs, mirror registries, or alternate distribution channels not counted in sig-004
$18k MRR claim → request Stripe or payment-processor revenue export for trailing 3 months
40% MoM growth claim → same revenue export with month-over-month cohort lines
10x agent-CI cost reduction → reproducible benchmark on one named design-partner workload with cache hit rate and $/run before/after
Design partners (Series-B devtools, AI lab) → two reference calls with permission, confirming production usage and spend saved
Prior fintech remote-cache background → one back-channel reference to prior employer infra lead
forum_trigger.non_empirical_crux: If routed checks reconcile to modest truth—smaller install base, lower MRR, benchmark holds but at early scale—does the fund's stated appetite for pre-track-record founders and shipping-velocity conviction justify the $100K fast check on execution evidence alone, or does unverified commercial traction remain a hard gate regardless of the written thesis? That posture fork survives even perfect diligence on the empirical claims.
